title: Court and interpreter friction module
transfer_mechanism: treat adjudication as an operating system with queue, staffing, and language-access failure modes
summary: Build a court-side module for backlog, interpreter demand, continuances, notice failure, and counsel access instead of leaving legal-system burden inside a generic 'other local costs' bucket.
caller_evidence: full-spectrum memo repeatedly flags court backlog and language barriers; no dedicated module exists

title: School service complexity index
transfer_mechanism: replace average dollars with complexity drivers like ELL, mobility, newcomer load, and grade concentration
summary: Move from blunt school spending to a district burden model built around ELL/newcomer intensity, child concentration, and service complexity.
caller_evidence: adversarial review explicitly says state school-spend is too blunt and points to ELL and SAIPE style variables

title: Labor-standards erosion module
transfer_mechanism: model employer abuse as aligned failures across payroll, classification, safety, and complaint systems
summary: Treat precarious-status labor as an institutional exploit surface that can widen wage theft, misclassification, payroll fraud, and injury externalities even when immigrant tax payment is nontrivial.
caller_evidence: full-spectrum memo already identifies this as real and under-modeled, with employer-side evidence on hand

title: Legal-regime delta ledger
transfer_mechanism: compare how local preemption, sanctuary rules, benefit rules, interpreter mandates, and school enrollment obligations either compartmentalize or propagate system load
summary: Model the same stock under different state and local legal stacks to see which burdens are caused by immigrant composition and which are caused by governance design.
caller_evidence: repo has governance claims and channel notes, but no law-stack matrix yet

title: Crime channel split beyond offending rates
transfer_mechanism: split offending, victimization, reporting, witness cooperation, clearance, and detention/adjudication burden into separate channels
summary: The repo already has a crime-rate memo. The missing question is whether low recorded offending still coexists with reporting frictions, witness noncooperation, or local justice-system burden.
caller_evidence: existing crime memo settles one channel, leaving downstream institutional channels mostly untouched

title: Institutional overload scorecard
transfer_mechanism: use trigger states, escalation tiers, and surge indicators rather than average annual burdens
summary: Define local overload indicators for schools, shelters, courts, transit, and hospitals, then ask which destinations cross trigger thresholds under which inflow profiles.
caller_evidence: institutional resilience is already the repo's stronger frame, but it lacks an operational scorecard

title: Stress-test scenario engine for local destinations
transfer_mechanism: evaluate local systems under adverse scenarios instead of only baseline means
summary: Run school, shelter, rent, interpreter, and hospital modules under adverse but plausible local scenarios: sudden inflow spike, housing freeze, recession, school bond failure, or federal rule change.
caller_evidence: current repo repeatedly says the sign depends on assumptions but does not yet operationalize local stress scenarios

title: Native adaptation and exit module
transfer_mechanism: invert the assumption that incumbent residents and firms stay put
summary: Track whether school crowding, rent pressure, or labor competition induce native relocation, private-school flight, commuting changes, or capital substitution that then changes the fiscal and political ledger.
caller_evidence: adversarial review says native relocation and capital response are not yet modeled directly

title: Political feedback module
transfer_mechanism: invert the assumption that policy preferences are exogenous to inflow and capacity stress
summary: Model whether inflow exposure changes support for redistribution, school bonds, policing, transit, or anti-immigration politics, and treat those shifts as downstream state-capacity variables rather than vibes.
caller_evidence: full-spectrum memo and AGI reframing both say backlash belongs in the main model

title: Assimilation velocity module
transfer_mechanism: invert the assumption that education, English, family structure, and earnings stay flat after arrival
summary: Model time-to-English, earnings growth, public-benefit exit, intermarriage, and legal-status transition to distinguish temporarily costly cohorts from persistently costly ones.
caller_evidence: important for long-run claims, but the repo has not yet surfaced a concrete failure trace demanding it now

title: Admission-channel composition layer
transfer_mechanism: decompose origin results into legal channel, family structure, and arrival pathway
summary: Use OHSS and related admin context to test whether current school-heavy or housing-heavy origin patterns are partly admission-channel patterns rather than origin traits.
caller_evidence: adversarial review explicitly warns that current origin-level findings may partly be admission-channel results

title: Housing code-enforcement branch as a standalone project
transfer_mechanism: invert the assumption that every missing housing subchannel deserves a separate branch
summary: A dedicated housing code-enforcement branch is possible, but right now it mostly duplicates the existing housing and local-burden stack unless paired with new administrative microdata.
caller_evidence: repo already has large housing coverage relative to courts, schools, and labor-law degradation
